Negative:

Backing into dock doors remove those metal brackets those damage the doors on trailers

Put your lumber north or south of parking lot for storage. That way truck drivers have room to back into docks.

Anything that is remotely nice in the store is considered Posh and cost way more. They sent an email prior to delivery to confirm the time frame and date of our delivery. We did have the option to decline and choose a different day and time if needed. We got a call 30 minutes prior to them coming to deliver which was very helpful. The company carried all the boxes down to our basement as requested. Highly recommend!","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Annamarie Moreland","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUQ_078PhA-lpynTESwb3Xk8NyCzGv7ooHPv3AHCcXkGk61CmpQxA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"From the moment that my merchandise hit their hands, they were in touch with me, several different ways.. They texted, they left voicemails. And they called to make sure I got all of those. And when they could give me a better delivery time of more exact. They did and even pushed me up ahead of the crowd that day.

I don't think you would have to worry about this delivery company. The box was intact and not damaged. The two delivery people brought in our marble heavy sink and put it inside the garage where we needed it.

I am excited if they are used again by any company to deliver to my home and feel confident that it comes undamaged and secure upon delivery. I have added pictures so you can see. There was no damage to the box or the inside contents. It could not have been light. The marble was heavy and it was solid wood. I would recommend this company.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDTtKyHXA&fid=0x0:0xcf173684601bc01c"],"When":"2024-5-23"},{"Name":"Chelly Thielke","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJgBl45iB2X1uQQeBjM-rj1R24vQoUn970NkqIV4AYY_Y6vXg4=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Erica went above and beyond to make sure our delivery was taken care of and over and above on the timeline in which their company delivered our dressers.

They were prompt, courteous and attentive to what we needed. Our box was in good condition as were the contents. We were very pleased with their service!","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Krista “K”","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVIk7n3M1cvaxkeciNwmJGhTF_25SZ_M9ZNFI7zvlbR8dNk_kZ3=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"We are so grateful to the movers and owner of Brouwer Relocation. They helped us tremendously - where PODS could not. Our cross-country move was interrupted as PODS could not take our 2 large PODS to our home/destination, but instead kept them in storage 6 hours away. We hired Brouwer Relocation to obtain our PODS, repack them into a semi and bring them home. It was done expertly - with care, efficiency and good spirits. This was a major full house move and we are extremely happy with the results. It took so much stress of us! In a world where moving is so complicated and stressful, we are confident in recommending Brouwer Relocation.","Images":null,"When":""}]

[{"Name":"John Devlin","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUFSph8Lj1VOlK4C7s_X0VY2nYot5jrjfJVzqnsprjnlIMp6T6hdw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Great place to shop and look at everything related to the American Dream. Guns, fishing, boating, camping, sports and equipment, archery, golf, and all the clothing needed for everything! High quality clothes! A little on the expensive side when it comes to home furnishings and art. Dog friendly! I love bringing my dog here. There is even a nice dinner spot and candy shop. The Ferris wheel is fun to ride as well!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDehsONyUGdFd84RImJkKQx&fid=0x0:0xcd373342d30de650"],"When":"2025-8-2"},{"Name":"Clark Holborn","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLtHQroHzwrFa6qO2Cn09_UKmasmSle1rEaJmPan7oEmpUkFA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"My background-21 years Marine Corps (retired), 65 y/o now after retired (again) from manufacturing industry as quality engineer.  I did some shopping online for a pair of running shoes and found a few pairs that I wanted to try on/purchase.  I was very careful to make sure that I was at the Sioux Falls Scheel's website and that I had selected "available at store".

I preface this review with all of that because the young man who helped me (and I use that phrase with hesitance) referred to me as a 'website warrior'; this after checking his inventory for me for a pair of shoes that I had researched (before driving 100 miles to get to the store) only to tell me that they weren't in stock.  I showed him the screenshot of the shoes from the website showing that they were listed as in stock and that's when I got the 'website warrior' thrown in my face as he suggested an apparent lack intelligence of people (maybe my age???) trying to successfully navigate the Scheels' website to accurately determine whether or not the item being requested was in stock at the store.  It's not rocket science to get around the Scheels website.  It's actually pretty intuitive.  But, hey, the shoes I selected might have been sold since the store opened this morning (I'm thinking) and so I asked about the next pair and I got the same response, "There aren't any in stock."  That's when I told the sales associate that my wife and I had driven from Watertown after careful review of the website and that I was a bit disappointed that I was striking out for a second time with my shoe choice.  He said that his inventory list was usually correct, but that he would check in the back.

And guess what???

The shoes that I'd told him were there, were actually there.  The shoes were great and I purchased them.

But...
I won't bother shopping for shoes again at Scheels after this experience.  "No biggy", you could be saying.  Except that my wife and I are very active "old" people who typically purchase more than a dozen pairs of shoes a year.

The Scheels in Sioux Falls is a great store.  We will probably continue to purchase our golf and hiking equipment from them in the future, but this experience has tarnished an otherwise shining example of how a brick and mortar business can succeed.

With annual retail purchases of more than 16% being made online by consumers (and continuing to rise) there is an increasing need for care and consideration to be given in the interactions between sales personnel and customers.  Customer service is the only significant difference between shopping online and running down to the store (even it's 100 miles away).  People enjoy being assisted in making the right choices when money seems to be losing its buying power.  Employees need to be trained.  Stocking levels need to be accurate, both on the devices being used by sales associates and on the website.  Otherwise, Amazon wins.  I don't want that.  I'm sure that you don't want that either.

With my sincere wishes for your success...","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Jake Anderson","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocL6gvmVM7KXEtMuQSAobWs_GfEoCvKM2rUpOnhvPQjHM35_0w=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I’ve been shopping here with friends and family ever since I was a kid. Plenty of great memories made here even if we didn’t purchase anything. They have the gear it takes to enjoy all of my passions and the employees are always super helpful.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Poppy","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWijeswN9vjdbywhD8hoGf7lvRKHFjBN7P5iKw7j55gxBkywQA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"This review is specifically for the incredibly knowledgeable young man working in the shoe department of the Sioux Falls store on the afternoon of Dec. 21. He dropped wild amounts of knowledge about all the shoe options and how they would work with certain foot/ankle issues, was attentive to customers, straightforward and honest. We ended up buying online because they were out of my size in store, but wanted to give shoe dude a shout out. Wish I would have gotten his name to give a proper one. It’s rare these days to meet someone doing customer service so well - he deserves a raise and/or a promotion!","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Patrick Falk","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKSrQKCzSWQFM3EmCT3M29BpXXwrqNoLd8AYRukIUFW94dwUg=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Had outstanding service from a young man in the men’s shoe department today. First name was Isaac and his last name was Long and hyphenated so I’m sorry I can’t be specific but I bet the store manager will be able to figure that out. Isaac was friendly, competent, and knowledgeable. Thank you for the great experience, Isaac!","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Nancy Blanco","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocL_MynnZkKprpE9SBLZBgGuzHWFeRv2df6HVGj8OW66u7yhoFej=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"We came to just hang out and explore. Kids were very excited about the ferris wheel. The young man working it was so kind. The only problem was while looking for shoes, not one associate greeted us or asked us if we needed any assistance. Found shoes on my own and got a few water toys, goggles, and a quiet ball for the kids. Other Scheels we visited were much more friendly.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Jes Scott","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWqBr1wcsUcaK5t1Lguqex0LX8P-mqHcRZUCFGP1HoKjLzXD6Aq=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I had a great experience with Scheels customer service. My local Scheels didn’t have my size in the coat I wanted, so I called the Sioux Falls Scheels and was helped right away. They arranged to ship the coat the very next day, which I truly appreciated.

When the package showed as delivered to a “box” but never arrived at my mailbox, I contacted Sioux Falls Scheels and spoke with Jennifer C. She immediately took ownership of the situation and went above and beyond to resolve it. Jennifer contacted another Scheels location and was able to have a replacement coat shipped out right away.

Her customer service was exceptional—professional, kind, and efficient. Jennifer C. is truly an asset to the Scheels team. Thank you, Jennifer, for turning a stressful situation into a positive experience.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Christopher Jordan","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXrUMYRaUTgYqUmTK723op8AGgMhQoQligF2Bj16rwmPUW39vtZ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Updated Review

I originally had a very disappointing experience ordering dog boots online for home delivery. I ordered two sizes since I wasn’t sure what would fit, and both pairs arrived with dog hair stuck to the Velcro and inside the packaging. While I understand customers may try items in-store, the returned products clearly hadn’t been inspected or cleaned before being shipped back out. Since the two items came from different locations (St. Cloud, MN and Sioux Falls, SD), it was clear this wasn’t an isolated oversight.

I’m updating my review because Josiah, the store manager in Sioux Falls, reached out to me directly after seeing my feedback and handled the situation exceptionally well. He took ownership, apologized, and resolved the issue quickly and professionally. His follow‑through—and the fact that the St. Cloud manager also reached out—showed that Scheels’ customer service is solid and consistent across locations.

Although my online order experience was frustrating, the way Josiah addressed the issue demonstrated a genuine commitment to customer satisfaction.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCUPoSdTgZcjFE-7NfWeXS8&fid=0x0:0xcd373342d30de650"],"When":"2026-2-10"}]

We flew and only did, maybe, half of the store. We were ready and checking out by 8:30–closing time—just in time! Cool thing was that even though we were there late, we weren’t being pushed out the door. Being so few people allowed us to move freely without people cutting us off. Thank you Costco for being open for us late workers!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDET42jjRQkTLTW3s8vU5xI&fid=0x0:0x7e32fb4c25d654a0"],"When":"2025-9-25"},{"Name":"Whitney Vos","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjX0MxqTIwsI7_hA25xfUy4FF5DWeUaDDtrqb4XKffg0fQ75lYE=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Wonderful costumer service! Always have loved shopping at Costco but tonight the manager & another employee from costumer service went above & beyond to fix a small cake mishap. I won’t go into detail but I am so thankful they are making this up to me so my dad can have the perfect cake for his birthday! Thank you!!! So appreciated","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Miles H.","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXSrVXFVBcyhU8CMhjYRYoEDmakeiz1E0twiVakUnnHpIDbjYdi6A=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Had a great experience at Costco. Colin N had to be the most helpful, and friendliest employee I have ever met! Answered our questions, and went so far above and beyond any of our expectations. He was SO helpful and incredible at his job. Highly recommend Costco, and Colin if you are going to need help!!","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Marc Sundstrom","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWWkUfKfm2omHGCkDnK4tZEI4Zi18oG6spJzqs7DON3b5CZwx9Y=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"This is one of my favorite places to buy organic produce, quality meats and cheeses, and a variety of other household items.  With a large family, Costco saves us some real money each month on groceries, and the 4% cash back at the end of the year (through the Costco Credit Card and Executive Membership) adds additional savings.  Best of all--no hassle returns on anything that doesn't meet their high quality standards.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"James Glenn","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjV1IB8LiS1uJ_C-Le4Cq5fY-1spJZJYrCQzxcgDtP706N8OQQY7-Q=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"We purchased a GE refrigerator from Costco online and had it installed by Costco Logistics on November 20, 2025. The installers chose to reuse an existing copper water line rather than the manufacturer-supplied line. The following day the line failed and leaked, causing water damage to our hardwood flooring and subfloor across our main floor.

Costco Logistics returned and replaced the copper line with the proper OEM line, confirming the initial installation was improper and prompted a new nation wide policy. To prevent mold and structural damage we hired a restoration company for drying and mitigation at the same cost of the refrigerator, which we paid.

This mitigation avoided a much larger flooring replacement that would have cost tens of thousands of dollars. We tried to handle this responsibly and minimize the loss.

The claim was routed through Costco Logistics to Gallagher Bassett and denied based on the copper line rather than the installation decision. Since November 2025 we have attempted multiple times to obtain escalation or resolution through Costco Logistics, corporate contacts, and in-store management without response as of February 2026.

We chose Costco specifically because of its reputation for standing behind its members. This experience has unfortunately been the opposite. We are simply requesting reimbursement of the mitigation cost and closure of the installation damage claim.

I hope Costco will review this and help resolve the issue.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Chris Davenport","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUUBEapAeZxuC0EA64Cv5uphBagLmnxqU4EGaFA-a6syabyPJk=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"Employees often congregate between the registers and food court and are often in the way of customers who are trying to leave the store. If it had happened only once, maybe twice, then whatever, I can easily let that go. I get in the way of people too or can be oblivious to my surroundings so I get it. But this has been a consistent issue when I shop there (we go weekly).","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Chris Henderson","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXP9DVHF8lwKN-MMi6i8FGt8HgD4AptAi8UP_i67fW7OMzN1Hg=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I love this location! My favorite thing is how restrictive they are to ensure paying customers are behaving correctly. For example, if you need to use the bathroom prior to your shopping, the guards at the exit door will stop you and make you scan your card at the entrance and walk all the way through the store and around the checkout lanes to do so. This is great, because hundreds of vagrants are just CRAWLING around this part of town trying to SCAM their way into bathrooms, and there are certainly no other businesses nearby with bathrooms they could try to use. If it weren't for this policy, the stalls would have lines out the door with paying customers waiting to use them! If it means a person with kidney dysfunction tinkles a little on the floor on the way, it's still worth it! He/she/they will have to hold it best they can until they make it through the store! It's also fantastic that they employ people at the exits who are hard-of-talking, as these people could never POSSIBLY be called upon to communicate with people verbally. I just LOVE the dedication to inclusion! Finally, it's great that if you present a shop card for payment they go to the trouble of first telling you that you are allowed to use it, even though you are clearly complying with all of the terms and conditions of its issuance. This makes customers feel welcomed and RESPECTED, and part of the EXCLUSIVE CLUB that is the Kirkland Signature shopping experience, where it's not like just anyone with $65 a year can get in! I love you, Costco!","Images":null,"When":""}]

[{"Name":"Kailey Harvey","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVNUlTltDik9TSvyNphmaY8vIbHMtylOUI2xMLvYia_GYsdDgWn=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"I am BEYOND upset about the experience I had with Sam’s club bakery. I hosted a baby shower 2/28, I called the Sam’s club bakery 2 weeks ago to place an order for cupcakes, I wanted the packs of gourmet cupcakes that they have sometimes but not always. 2 years ago I had done the same thing for my wedding and the day of they told me they had no order in the book for my cupcakes. The same thing happened to me today. When placing the order I let the lady know what happened to me on my wedding day and I was already hesitant with ordering because I did not want it to happen again, sure enough. The are not reliable and I will NEVER order from here again considering the lack of accountability and reliability. The lady I had spoke to today did tell me they could make 6 packs (I ordered 8) but I would have to pick them up at 1 o’clock, my party started at 1 and I had place the original pick up time to be 10:30. Do better, I won’t ruin my events next time and order somewhere else","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Briana Vudi","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocL2L6F3yaKJtbtlPVvekdA_mU3xIZ8_TXkLMK55AnlaoGT2Gg=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":2,"Description":"Ordered pickup to make it easier on my husband to grab the couple things we needed. Dog food and fairlife nutrition drinks. Fairlife was outed on the order…. Same spot they always are","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhB-IlFikcOx3esxlDpMZb_H&fid=0x0:0x75bd05855c44c907"],"When":"2025-10-11"},{"Name":"Josh","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXnHuK46KqNhtqZCQnBj-Ybil-zDbrEpLcxC_npSlwHKNP8LhFy=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"Sams club. Welp...short version, trailer park costco.

Costco- way more organized, product placement, easy to find what your looking for, even checking out theres an order to it. The employees are friendlier and ask if you need help. Rude snobby white woman throughout store.

Samsclub- zero organization, like someone took bulk walmart items and threw them on a random shelf. Check out was a nightmare, theres no organized line....just a free for all. I dont think I saw an employee except at checkout, and they weren't very helpful. Was quicker leaving with that scanner machine.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Priscilla Quintana","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocK7HDSIqWAUMqD_XB8iObImUjcvKWtsmTThdVc4VBbAOI9mFQ=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I wanted to take a moment to highly commend Leonard for the truly above-and-beyond service he provided tonight.

Despite the late hour and the fact that he was clearly busy wrapping things up, he immediately dropped everything he was doing, and retrieved it for me with a positive attitude.
In a world where customer service often feels rushed, it was so refreshing to see someone prioritize the member's needs and show such genuine helpfulness, especially so close to closing time. He exemplified what great customer service is all about.
Thank you again Leonard for the exceptional effort and kindness!","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Paul Donahue","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLatIMJXvnBQdHGOUW19KGZR41O5z9w-2C7OnDzMg8YBi1EIA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"The Sam's Club in Sioux Falls is fantastic! Highly recommend for both personal and business shopping!","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Sandy L","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIvyR2ietwLCIaKfrsLrgWycasslQjZe8le3zHNNS6j9h12CQ=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I want to acknowledge the above and beyond service I received from Kay in bakery! user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Melissa Lynn","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVAgiLiIImrVGhBF_cGmXdmlKGgtRdjBOBYSFF9ow7ZXw3paCdxJw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"It used to be a great place. Now it's over priced,  out dated food comparable to Walmart prices. Very close. You can do better at Dollar Tree. You can find some interesting products,  they buy pallets from all over the US, but the prices keep creeping up and up and much of the food is near date or out of date.  It'd be fine, but the ingredients in jars are separated,  discolored. Out date, near out of date. I didn't mind, when it was half or less than half retail,  but that was when they FIRST opened.
Go look for yourself.
They sell tons of things at such an overpriced rate.
A dented can of peas for .80 cents?. Keep it. The place is off my rotation now. They should be ashamed of themselves.  Ripping folks off, with out dated, improperly stored food for just pennies cheaper (if at all) than a regular store.
Here are 22 pics of some of what I saw today
Pics all taken at new location on Minnesota on January 2nd, 2025
You can get an idea of some things they have and how these items are priced and presented
It's not affordable,  when they offer ZERO refunds and so many things are expired, but like not just by date, also by quality.
I can't believe they're ev2nnin business.  Surely, people deserve better.  Just shop sales are regular stores and use this place if you want to find something more unusual or exotic... or cheap, cheap hostess goods. They have so many of those individual cake things, they could give them away and provide snacks for homeless or starving,  but they shouldnt charge money for them!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICf_-KiOg&fid=0x0:0xe5e8b98647482482"],"When":"2025-1-3"},{"Name":"Tim Farnsworth","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUI2MQ0coqGw8xHggmHtEVmVnfy9o8gfQYpWfkaQFmSSHaMtxziTg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"This is updated with new location

Staff is really friendly. Good prices on items
They do continue with the pricing they had at old location. Feels a little like a regular grocery store. But stock is a little scattered. .

Make sure you check expiration dates. It is a very large building and different then the old setup. Stock is hit and miss. Changes constantly. Used to be nice when they were on the west side. And east side.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIC_0of8Fw&fid=0x0:0xe5e8b98647482482"],"When":"2025-1-14"},{"Name":"Speedy","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWBi_I_4mOjI9qOc1zMzQghW8tlVJrkn6sSdJtWXsj0-tz44XEM=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"Cheapest cereal in town!! They have alot of great deals on everything, especially cereal and pet food/supplies. From my understanding, they have had a lot of theft and centralizing their store to one location I'm sure has helped with that. But the prices still just don't seem to be realistic when it comes to their mission in providing a good deal. Sure it beats retail but what am I really benefiting paying between 20%-40% less but am gambling if it's going to realistically be healthy to eat with genuinely no harmful side affects. Like if it's a product that's stored in plastic, how do we know it hasn't been out in the sun? Is there legitimate quality control going on... My shopping experience continues to dwindle because their prices go up and quality/quantity doesn't seem to reflect that. I understand this is a challenging industry but some of the things I've seen in the shelves here are just unreal and they'll sit their for weeks... Finishing my point about saving a buck. So if we are buying outdated product and let's say about 10% of what we buy is actually gross and not even really usable, what are we really saving. Don't get me wrong, the benefits of this place outweigh the negatives tenfold. But it's up to the consumer to be absolutely precise with their shopping decisions or this place can quickly be just as expensive as a regular retailer when you factor everything I just said. Be diligent when you come her and don't take everything with a grain of salt. Especially the sauces. If I were to do the math on my entire purchase history here which is well into the thousands, I'm sure my savings versus a retail spender would be in the range of 30% but that comes with having to diligently check products while also having to basically kind of trial thing for the first year to learn how they price things according to how they'll actually be. 🫣","Images":null,"When":""}]
